title: Essential Skills for Your Future: Career & Life Vocabulary Pack
description: This vocabulary pack introduces 6th-8th grade ELL newcomer students to key terms related to career exploration and essential life skills. Content is designed to be age-appropriate and supports understanding of future planning and personal development.

vocabulary.image_prompt: A student completing homework independently, a person taking care of a pet, or someone cleaning up a spill.
A group of diverse students collaborating on a project, building a tower together, or playing a sport as a team.
A student looking at a puzzle or a broken object with thought bubbles showing different solutions, a person fixing a flat tire.
A student writing down future plans in a notebook, an arrow hitting a target, or a person climbing steps towards a flag.
Two students talking and listening attentively, speech bubbles connecting people, or someone giving a presentation.
A person looking at a chart or spreadsheet with income and expenses, coins in different labeled jars (savings, spending).
A winding road with different career icons (doctor, artist, engineer) along the way, a person looking at a map with various job options.
A student with a question mark and lightbulb over their head, examining information closely, or comparing two different ideas.
A person standing at a crossroads with two different paths, a scale balancing two options, or someone choosing between two items.
A student looking at a schedule or calendar, a clock with different activities marked around it, or someone juggling multiple tasks efficiently.
Two people sitting opposite each other at a desk, one asking questions and the other answering, a professional handshake.
A document showing sections for 'Experience,' 'Education,' and 'Skills,' a person handing a paper to another person.
A young person selling lemonade, someone drawing a business plan on a whiteboard, or a lightbulb with money symbols around it.
A student counting money, a piggy bank, or a person looking at a graph showing savings growth.
Several hands reaching into the center to form a circle, people working together on a shared document or puzzle.

worksheets.instructions_l2: Une cada palabra de vocabulario con su definición correcta. Traza una línea desde la palabra hasta su definición.

worksheets.instructions_en: Choose 5 vocabulary words from this pack. For each word, write one sentence explaining how this skill could help you in school or in a future job.
worksheets.instructions_l2: Elige 5 palabras de vocabulario de este paquete. Para cada palabra, escribe una oración explicando cómo esta habilidad podría ayudarte en la escuela o en un trabajo futuro.

worksheets.instructions_en: Sort the following life skills into the correct category. Write each word under the heading where it best fits.
worksheets.instructions_l2: Clasifica las siguientes habilidades para la vida en la categoría correcta. Escribe cada palabra debajo del encabezado donde mejor corresponda.

items.content: Words to sort: Responsibility, Teamwork, Problem-solving, Goal Setting, Communication, Budgeting, Career Path, Critical Thinking, Decision Making, Time Management, Interview, Resume, Entrepreneurship, Financial Literacy, Collaboration

items.correct_answer: Responsibility, Goal Setting, Critical Thinking, Decision Making, Time Management
Career Path, Interview, Resume, Entrepreneurship, Problem-solving

pages.text_l2: El Viaje de Leo: Desarrollando Habilidades para la Vida

pages.text_en: Leo, a middle school student, often dreamed about his future. He knew he wanted to do something exciting, but he wasn't sure how to start. His teacher, Ms. Elena, told him, “To build a strong future, you need strong life skills!”
pages.text_l2: Leo, un estudiante de secundaria, a menudo soñaba con su futuro. Sabía que quería hacer algo emocionante, pero no estaba seguro de cómo empezar. Su maestra, la Sra. Elena, le dijo: “¡Para construir un futuro sólido, necesitas habilidades para la vida sólidas!”

pages.text_en: First, Ms. Elena taught them about **Goal Setting**. “What do you want to achieve?” she asked. Leo learned to set small, achievable goals. He also learned about **Responsibility**, understanding that reaching goals requires taking ownership of his tasks.
pages.text_l2: Primero, la Sra. Elena les enseñó sobre el **Establecimiento de Metas**. “¿Qué quieren lograr?” preguntó ella. Leo aprendió a establecer metas pequeñas y alcanzables. También aprendió sobre la **Responsabilidad**, comprendiendo que alcanzar las metas requiere asumir la propiedad de sus tareas.

pages.text_en: Next, they worked on a big group project. Leo practiced **Teamwork** and **Collaboration**, learning to work effectively with his classmates. He also improved his **Communication** skills, sharing ideas clearly and listening to others.
pages.text_l2: Luego, trabajaron en un gran proyecto grupal. Leo practicó el **Trabajo en equipo** y la **Colaboración**, aprendiendo a trabajar eficazmente con sus compañeros. También mejoró sus habilidades de **Comunicación**, compartiendo ideas claramente y escuchando a los demás.

pages.text_en: When their project faced unexpected problems, Leo used his **Problem-solving** skills. He applied **Critical Thinking** to analyze the situation and make a good **Decision Making** with his team.
pages.text_l2: Cuando su proyecto enfrentó problemas inesperados, Leo usó sus habilidades de **Resolución de problemas**. Aplicó el **Pensamiento crítico** para analizar la situación y tomar una buena **Toma de decisiones** con su equipo.

pages.text_en: Ms. Elena also taught them about **Financial Literacy**. Leo learned about **Budgeting** his allowance, saving money for things he wanted, and understanding how money works. He even started thinking about different **Career Paths**.
pages.text_l2: La Sra. Elena también les enseñó sobre **Educación financiera**. Leo aprendió sobre cómo hacer un **Presupuesto** con su mesada, ahorrar dinero para las cosas que quería y entender cómo funciona el dinero. Incluso comenzó a pensar en diferentes **Trayectorias profesionales**.

pages.text_en: To prepare for future jobs, they practiced mock **Interview** skills and learned how to create a simple **Resume**. Leo also explored the idea of **Entrepreneurship**, thinking about starting his own small business one day.
pages.text_l2: Para prepararse para futuros trabajos, practicaron habilidades de **Entrevista** simuladas y aprendieron a crear un **Currículum vitae** simple. Leo también exploró la idea del **Emprendimiento**, pensando en iniciar su propio pequeño negocio algún día.

pages.text_en: Leo realized that these 'life skills' weren't just for adults. They were essential for success in school, in his community, and for his exciting future! He felt more prepared and confident than ever.
pages.text_l2: Leo se dio cuenta de que estas 'habilidades para la vida' no eran solo para adultos. ¡Eran esenciales para el éxito en la escuela, en su comunidad y para su emocionante futuro! Se sintió más preparado y seguro que nunca.

teacher_notes.objective: Students will be able to identify, define, and use key vocabulary related to career exploration and essential life skills, enhancing their understanding of personal development and future planning.
teacher_notes.suggested_use: Introduce vocabulary using flashcards and visuals. Read the mini-book aloud, pausing to discuss vocabulary in context. Use matching and tracing worksheets for reinforcement. The coloring worksheet can serve as a creative assessment. Encourage students to use the words in sentences and discuss how these skills apply to their daily lives and future aspirations.
materials_needed: Printed vocabulary cards (with English, Spanish, and image prompts)
Mini-book copies for each student or one large copy for shared reading
Whiteboard or projector for introducing new words
differentiation_tips: **For struggling learners:** Provide sentence starters for discussions. Allow students to use bilingual dictionaries. Pair them with a stronger English speaker for activities. Focus on a smaller set of vocabulary words if needed.

**For advanced learners:** Encourage them to write short paragraphs or journal entries using the vocabulary. Have them research specific careers and identify which life skills are most important for those roles. Ask them to create their own definitions or example sentences.

answers.3: Teamwork / Trabajo en equipo OR Collaboration / Colaboración
answers.4: Budgeting / Presupuesto OR Financial Literacy / Educación financiera
